data.title: US crude stockpiles rise, gasoline and distillate draw down - EIA
data.description:
U.S. crude oil stockpiles rose while gasoline and distillate inventories fell last week as seasonal maintenance at refineries led to lower processing, the Energy Information Administration said on Thursday.

Crude inventories rose by 4.6 million barrels to 432.5 million barrels in the week ended February 14, the EIA said, compared with analysts' expectations in a Reuters poll for a 3.1 million-barrel build.

Crude stocks at the Cushing, Oklahoma, delivery hub (USOICC=ECI) rose by 1.5 million barrels last week.

U.S. and global crude futures extended gains slightly following the report. West Texas Intermediate crude (WTI) was trading up 75 cents at $73 a barrel by 12:14 p.m. EST (1314 GMT), while Brent rose 90 cents to $76.93.

"The low refinery run rate is still contributing to this backup in crude oil supplies," said John Kilduff, a partner at Again Capital in New York, adding that the low utilization was also contributing to easing gasoline inventories.

"Refineries are just not cranking out as much gasoline as we would have expected."

Refinery crude runs (USOICR=ECI) fell by 15,000 barrels per day (bpd), and utilization rates (USOIRU=ECI) fell by 0.1 percentage point in the week to 84.9% of total capacity.

Gasoline stocks (USOILG=ECI) fell by 151,000 barrels to 247.9 million barrels, the EIA said, compared with expectations for a 6,000-barrel draw.

Distillate stockpiles (USOILD=ECI), which include diesel and heating oil, fell by 2.1 million barrels to 116.6 million barrels, versus forecasts for a 1.6 million-barrel drop, the EIA data showed.

Net U.S. crude imports (USOICI=ECI) fell last week by 961,000 bpd, and exports rose 472,000 bpd, the EIA said.

data.title: Daiwa Comments on Thursday's Germany Bunds, U.K. Gilts
data.description:
German Bunds followed United States Treasuries higher on Thursday even as eurozone consumer confidence rose to a four-month high in February and construction output provided modest support to Q4 gross domestic product growth, said Daiwa Capital Markets.

February's flash PMIs will provide the focus on Friday in the eurozone, noted the bank. A further modest improvement should be expected after January's activity indices showed signs of an upturn, with the headline eurozone composite PMI up 0.6pt to an eight-month high of 50.2.

Admittedly, this was still merely consistent with stagnant output rather than expansion, while growth in services was reportedly a touch softer (51.3). But the detail for the beleaguered manufacturing sector hinted that the pace of deterioration had at least started to moderate, with the respective output index up to a nine-month high (47.1).

The composite input cost PMI also rose to 58.3, the highest level since May 2023. But lower oil prices should help keep a lid on input cost gauges this month, while the comparably sanguine output prices indices (52.9) continue to suggest that firms are largely absorbing such pressures within their margins.

Elsewhere, INSEE's business sentiment surveys, which can often provide a more accurate signposting for French economic activity, are also due. Ahead of Monday's release of final eurozone inflation estimates for January, the equivalent Italian inflation figures are also due on Friday. The flash release showed the headline Italian HICP rate rising 0.3ppt to 1.7% year over year, a 15-month high, primarily due to an increase in regulated energy prices.

Shorter-dated Gilts also made modest gains as a United Kingdom manufacturing survey signaled ongoing contraction in the three months to February, added Daiwa.

Having slipped back in three out of the four months up to December, retail sales are expected to report a rebound at the start of the year, pointed out Daiwa. Indeed, while the CBI distributive trades survey proved underwhelming in January, a separate survey from the BRC flagged a more marked improvement, with expectations for sales growth of 1/2%M/M or more.

But Friday's GfK consumer sentiment survey is likely to point to a subdued outlook for consumer spending — indeed, a separate BRC on Thursday suggested that consumer expectations for the coming three months worsened in February for a fifth consecutive month. But arguably of greatest importance on Friday will be February's flash PMIs, which are likely again to be consistent with broad stagnation.

The composite PMI should be little changed from 50.6 in January, with the contraction in manufacturing output only offset by modest services growth. Indeed, Thursday's CBI industrial trends survey suggested that production fell in the three months to February at a similar pace to January and in 16 out of 17 subsectors.

While a notable decline in stock adequacy might point to a pickup in production ahead, order books remained well below average.

Finally, Friday's public finance data will also be of particular interest, owing to January's self-assessment deadlines. So, tax revenues will have a bearing on whether corrective fiscal measures will eventually be required to ensure consistency with the Government's fiscal rules, according to Daiwa.

data.title: ST LOUIS FED'S MUSALEM Q&A/ECNY: NET EFFECT OF HIGHER IMMIGRATION A BIGGER ECONOMY; IMMIGRATION DECLINED TOWARD END OF BIDEN ADMIN, STILL HEADING DOWN
Home Sales Are Expected to Be Dismal. Watch This Data Instead. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Shaina Mishkin

Home sales data expected Friday will reflect old news: home buyers and sellers had a tough winter. Investors should keep their eyes on mortgage applications instead.

Existing-home sales in January likely sagged to the slowest sales pace since October, according to FactSet consensus estimates. Economists expect that homes were sold at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.1 million, down 3.3% from the previous month. The National Association of Realtors is scheduled to publish the data on Friday at 10 a.m.

The measure is the main way analysts, economists, and industry professionals assess historic trends in the national housing resale market, which comprises a much bigger share of all sales than new construction. Since it can take a month or two for home sales to close, the figure reflects completed sales that went under contract a month or two prior.

That time gap feels particularly wide as the industry looks for signs of strength ahead of the spring. The expected drop in sales will largely reflect homes that went under contract at a time when mortgage rates were rising quickly, adding to the cost of buying a home.

Mortgage rates have steadied in recent weeks, though they remain at levels near 7%, according to Freddie Mac. The average 30-year mortgage rate this week was 6.85%. "The 30-year fixed-rate mortgage has stayed just under 7% for five consecutive weeks and in that time has fluctuated less than 20 basis points," Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist, said in a statement. "This stability continues to bode well for potential buyers and sellers as we approach the spring homebuying season."

Some real estate agents have reported early signs of buyer demand ahead of the spring season — but whether those signs translate into a significant pickup in sales has yet to be seen. Weekly applications for home purchase loans, a leading indicator of buyer demand published by the Mortgage Bankers Association, dropped a seasonally adjusted 6% the week of Feb. 14, from the week prior. The index remains at a level well below the seasonally adjusted long-term average.

Coming trends for the spring will be reflected first in mortgage application data before trickling down into closed home sales measures. Investors should keep their eyes on the trade group's weekly index, which is released on Wednesday mornings, to monitor the start of the season in coming weeks.

Write to Shaina Mishkin at shaina.mishkin@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: FTC Release: Federal Trade Commission Launches Inquiry on Tech Censorship
data.description:
Today, the Federal Trade Commission launched a public inquiry to better understand how technology platforms deny or degrade users' access to services based on the content of their speech or affiliations, and how this conduct may have violated the law.

View Press Release: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2025/02/federal-trade-commission-launches-inquiry-tech-censorship

data.title: TD Bank Data Breach – Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Launches Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / TD Bank recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal data of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal information entrusted to TD Bank.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

TD Bank recently became aware of a security incident on its IT network. In response, TD Bank launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. Upon investigation TD Bank determined that a former employee had unauthorized access to sensitive customer data from August 2022 to December 2022 and may have accessed and shared individuals' personal information. TD Bank conducted a comprehensive review of the impacted data to determine what information was compromised and how many individuals were impacted.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:full names,contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses), birth dates, account numbers and transaction details.On January 21, 2025, TD Bank filed a notice with the Massachusetts state government and started sending out notice letters to the affected individuals.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from TD Bank, you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

Levi Korsinsky, LLP is investigating whether affected individuals are entitled to compensation. If you have received a notice about the data breach, you may be entitled to compensation. There is no cost or obligation to participate. Follow the link below to find out:

https://tinyurl.com/4jv72zv5

data.title: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Investigates Grubhub Holdings Inc. Data Breach
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Grubhub Holdings Inc. recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal data of consumers, drivers, and merchants. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal information entrusted to Grubhub.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

If you received a letter or email from Grubhub Holdings Inc. ("Grubhub") concerning a data breach that exposed your personal information, you may be entitled to compensation. The data breach exposed the information of consumers, drivers, and merchants. For consumers and drivers, personal information was exposed, including names, email addresses, phone numbers, hashed passwords, and partial payment information. Merchants also had information exposed some payment card details, includingthe type of card and last four digits of payment cards. The data breach was only recently made public but was likely known to Grubhub for months. The data breach is thought to have impacted an unknown number of Grubhub customers, drivers, and merchants.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

data.title: INVESTIGATION ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Announces Investigation of New York Blood Center Enterprises Data Breach
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / New York Blood Center Enterprises recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal and protected health data of 193,306 individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal and protected health information entrusted to NYBC.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On January 26, 2025, NYBC discovered that it was targeted in a ransomware attack after detecting suspicious activity on its IT systems. In response, NYBC immediately launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. Upon investigation NYBC determined that a ransomware attack affected their IT systems and may have viewed and obtained sensitive personal and protected health information of individuals. NYBC is still working on restoring its systems to full functionality and determining if any confidential information was accessed or obtained during the attack.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal and protected health information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:names, dates of birth, addresses, Social Security number, phone numbers, email addresses, and health information.NYBC made a public disclosure but has not sent out data breach notification letters as they are still investigating whether sensitive data was compromised.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from NYBC,you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

data.title: San Francisco-Marin Food Bank Data Breach under Investigation by Levi & Korsinsky, LLP
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / San Francisco-Marin Food Bank recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal data of thousands of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal information entrusted to San Francisco-Marin Food Bank.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On May 31, 2024, San Francisco-Marin Food Bank became aware of a security incident on its IT network. In response, the organization immediately launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. Upon investigation, San Francisco-Marin Food Bank determined that an unauthorized third party gained access to its network between May 10, 2024, and May 30, 2024, and may have acquired certain files. The organization conducted a comprehensive review of the impacted data to determine what information was compromised and how many individuals were impacted. On January 7, 2025, the organization completed its review and began confirming address information to notify individuals.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach: name, date of birth and Social Security number.On February 4, 2025, San Francisco-Marin Food Bank filed a notice with the Maine Attorney General's Office and started sending out notice letters to the affected individuals.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from San Francisco-Marin Food Bank, you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

data.title: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Investigates Lucent Health Solutions, LLC Data Breach
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Lucent Health Solutions, LLC recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal and protected health data of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal and protected health information entrusted to Lucent Health.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On October 2, 2023, a Lucent Health manager opened a single "phishing" email from a trusted broker. Lucent Health's IT team identified suspicious activity on the employee's email account and eliminated access within approximately ninety minutes. In response, Lucent Health launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. Upon investigation, Lucent Health determined that one e-mail account was subject to unauthorized access for a limited period (approximately ninety (90) minutes), and that the unauthorized individual queried select search terms. Lucent Health conducted a comprehensive review of the impacted data to determine what information was compromised and how many individuals were impacted.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal and protected health information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:full name, date of birth, Social Security number, and health, dental, and vision group and/or plan number.On January 30, 2025, Lucent Health reported the incident to the Attorney General of California and started sending out notice letters to the affected individuals.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from Lucent Health,you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

data.title: Endo Group, LLC d/b/a Endo Group, Garden City SurgiCenter, and Syosset SurgiCenter Data Breach - Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Launches Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Endo Group, LLC d/b/a Endo Group, Garden City SurgiCenter, and Syosset Surgi Center recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal and protected health data of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal and protected health information entrusted to Endo Group.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On October 1, 2024, Endo Group discovered a security incident on its network. In response, Endo Group launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. On December 31, 2024, upon investigation, Endo Group determined that an unauthorized third party had accessed two Endo Group email accounts, between April 15, 2024, and July 18, 2024, and had accessed certain personal information related to its patients and a limited number of employees. Endo Group conducted a comprehensive review of the impacted data to determine what information was compromised and how many individuals were impacted.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal and protected health information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:name, Social Security number, date of birth, contact information, driver's license or other ID number, credit card or financial account information, medical information (e.g., name of treatment facility, name of healthcare providers, medical diagnosis and treatment information, medical record number, dates and times of service), and health insurance and payment information.On January 27, 2025, Endo Group made a public disclosure and started sending out notice letters and emails to the affected individuals.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from Endo Group,you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

Levi Korsinsky, LLP is investigating whether affected individuals are entitled to compensation. If you have received a notice about the data breach, you may be entitled to compensation. There is no cost or obligation to participate. Follow the link below to find out:

data.title: INVESTIGATION ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Announces Investigation of Lighthouse Electric Company Data Breach
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Lighthouse Electric Company recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal and protected health data of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal and protected health information entrusted to the LEC.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On October 26, 2024, LEC became aware of a security incident on its IT network. In response, LEC launched an investigation to determine the nature and scope of the incident. Upon investigation, LEC determined an unauthorized third party gained access to its network and copied certain files without authorization between October 21, 2024, and October 26, 2024. LEC conducted a comprehensive review of the affected files to identify the types of information contained and to whom the information relates. On December 23, 2024, LEC completed its review and determined that the affected files contained certain individuals' personal information.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal and protected health information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:name, Social Security number, bank account information, health insurance information.On February 4, 2025, LEC filed a notice with the Maine Attorney General's Office and started sending out notice letters to the affected individuals.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

data.title: University Diagnostic Medical Imaging, PC Data Breach - Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Launches Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / University Diagnostic Medical Imaging, PC recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal and protected health data of thousands of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal and protected health information entrusted to UDMI.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

On November 26, 2024, University Diagnostic Medical Imaging, PC became aware of a security incident affecting its computer network. Upon detection, UDMI launched an investigation with the assistance of third-party cybersecurity experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. The investigation determined that an unauthorized third party gained access to certain files and folders containing confidential patient information for a limited amount of time on November 26, 2024.

Upon information and belief, the following types of sensitive personal and protected health information may have been compromised as a result of the data breach:name, address, date of birth, referring physician, and/or medical treatment/diagnosis information. On January 21, 2025, UDMI filed a notice with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights (OCR). UDMI is expected to send out data breach notification letters to impacted individuals soon.Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY AM I ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

data.title: Euro zone bond yields dip from three-week high, defence spending in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Moves in euro zone bond yields muted
  • European defence spending still in focus
  • Traders pared ECB rate cut expectations on Wednesday

Longer-dated euro zone bond yields dipped from around their highest in three weeks on Thursday, while geopolitics remained in focus and markets awaited more clarity around the prospects of increased defence spending in Europe.

U.S. President Donald Trump denounced Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy as a "dictator" on Wednesday and warned he had to move quickly to secure peace or risk losing his country.

Germany's 10-year bond yield (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for the euro zone bloc, was last down 1 basis point (bp) at 2.536%, after earlier hitting its highest since January 30 at 2.563%. Yields move inversely to prices.

Euro zone bonds have sold off this week on expectations that European governments would ramp up issuance to fund bigger defence spending.

While bond moves were muted on Thursday, 10-year German Bund yields were on track for their biggest weekly jump since early January, having risen about 11 bps on the week.

"The politicians seem to realise that Europe needs to prepare for a situation where it cannot rely on the U.S. when it comes to how much responsibility they will take for defending Europe in the coming years," said Jussi Hiljanen, head of European rates strategy at SEB.

"It's a new playing field altogether. It's going to be reflected in the funding needs."

Hiljanen said however that there might be room for a downwards correction of the past days' rise in longer-dated euro zone yields, saying the moves were "maybe a bit exaggerated".

Italy's 10-year yield was down 2 bps at 3.618%, after touching its highest since January 29 on Wednesday at 3.644%.

The gap between Italian and German yields (DE10IT10=RR) stood at 108 bps.

Traders also assessed minutes from the U.S. Federal Reserve's January policy meeting, published on Wednesday, showing that Fed officials discussed slowing or pausing the ongoing drawdown of its balance sheet holdings. This sent U.S. Treasury yields down.

PARED ECB BETS

Germany's two-year bond yield (DE2YT=RR), which is more sensitive to European Central Bank rate expectations, fell 3 bps to 2.148%.

It rose 4 bps on Wednesday after ECB board member Isabel Schnabel told the Financial Times that the central bank should start a discussion about whether further rate cuts are necessary.

"Coming from her, such remarks should not surprise as Schnabel has previously been estimating the neutral rate as high as 3%," said ING analysts in a note.

"But what has changed in the meantime is the perception of the fiscal backdrop, where aside from the immediate supply implications, the prospect for larger defence investments also argues for a more expansionary stance ahead," they added.

The ECB sees the neutral rate, which neither stimulates nor restricts growth, at between 1.75% and 2.25%. The bank's deposit rate is currently 2.75%.

Also in the mix, German producer prices rose less than expected in January, increasing by 0.5% on the year, the federal statistics office reported on Thursday. Analysts polled by Reuters had expected a 1.3% increase.

data.title: Canada's Teck sees no material impact from Trump tariffs
data.description:
Canadian miner Teck Resources said on Thursday U.S. President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs on Canadian imports will not have any material impact on its business.

"Our copper and zinc concentrate sales would not be impacted as we primarily sell to Asia and Europe and not into the U.S.," CEO Jonathan Price said on a post-earnings call.

The company does sell some products in the US – mainly zinc and lead refined at its Trail operations in British Columbia, as well as speciality metals such as germanium, indium and sulfur.

While Teck produces a large proportion of these diverse group of metals, they comprise less than 15% of the company's revenue, Price said.

"In the event tariffs are imposed, we expect trade flows to adjust," he added.

Teck shares were trading nearly 2% higher midday after the miner beat analysts' estimates for fourth-quarter profit, helped by higher copper production volumes at its Chile mine.

Total copper output in the reported quarter came in at 122,100 tonnes, a 19% jump from a year earlier, with 60,700 tonnes from its Quebrada Blanca (QB) mine in Chile.

Strong sales volumes and favourable prices of the red metal also supported.

The company has been investing in the QB debottlenecking project and expects it could lead to a further increase in throughput by 10-15%.

Teck expects copper production to range between 490,000 and 565,000 tonnes in 2025.

The company reported an adjusted profit of C$0.45 ($0.3168) per share for the quarter ended December 31, compared with analysts' average estimate of C$0.43 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 1.4205 Canadian dollars)
